objective: Add an implementation-ready plan for an ai-integration-services Local CI Gate profile owned by hldpro-governance so AIS can become the second live consumer of the reusable profile/deployer model.
scope_boundary: Keep hldpro-governance as the canonical source for Local CI Gate runner logic and repo-specific profiles.
Add an ai-integration-services profile under tools/local-ci-gate/profiles/ that invokes commands already present in AIS.
Keep reports local-only under cache/local-ci-gate/reports/ in consumer repos.
Document the AIS profile and future managed-shim update path in the governance runbook.
Validate the profile against an isolated AIS worktree before proposing consumer rollout.
out_of_scope: Changing ai-integration-services product behavior in this governance issue.
Copying Local CI Gate runner code into AIS.
Adding new AIS npm scripts from this governance PR.
Replacing GitHub Actions or claiming local checks are a full CI replay.
Installing a managed AIS shim before the governance profile is merged and dogfooded.
research_summary: AIS was selected as the next consumer after knocktracker because it has a different repo shape and existing deterministic root scripts. Read-only inspection found candidate commands including typecheck, preflight, probe:push, audit:error-handlers, app builds, smoke, and test. The shared AIS checkout is dirty, so implementation must use an isolated AIS worktree before live dogfood.

sprints.name: Planning and Scope Bootstrap
sprints.goal: Land trusted planning and implementation scope artifacts before any AIS Local CI Gate profile implementation.
tasks: Create an issue-backed structured plan for #264.
Add planning and implementation execution-scope artifacts.
Add PDCA/R and planning cross-review artifacts for traceability.
Update OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md and docs/PROGRESS.md so #260 is no longer active and #264 is visible.
Validate structured plans, backlog alignment, diff hygiene, and execution-scope write boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: GitHub issue #264 is open and referenced by governance roadmap/progress mirrors.
Planning PR contains only planning/scope/cross-review/status artifacts.
Implementation branch and allowed write paths are declared before implementation starts.
The planning PR does not change runner behavior, deployer behavior, profile behavior, or downstream repos.

sprints.name: Profile Implementation
sprints.goal: Add and validate the AIS Local CI Gate profile without changing AIS code.
tasks: Create tools/local-ci-gate/profiles/ai-integration-services.yml with changed-file scoped checks mapped to existing AIS commands.
Add profile validation coverage so bundled profiles stay parseable and preserve the report/disclaimer contract.
Document how AIS can install a managed shim after the profile lands.
Create or use an isolated AIS worktree for dry-run and live dogfood.
acceptance_criteria: The AIS profile loads through the existing runner.
Dry-run output prints planned AIS checks and the CI-authoritative disclaimer.
Live run executes deterministic checks that exist in AIS when dependencies are installed.
Heavy builds/tests are changed-file scoped where practical.
A follow-up AIS PR can install a managed shim using the AIS profile.

specialist_reviews.focus: Read-only inspection of AIS package scripts and governance rules before profile planning.
specialist_reviews.summary: AIS exposes enough existing deterministic root scripts for a profile, but the shared checkout is dirty and must not be used for writes.

specialist_reviews.focus: Mirror the issue #260 planning-to-implementation flow for issue #264.
specialist_reviews.summary: The same two-PR pattern applies: trusted planning/scope first, then implementation branch.

alternate_model_review.summary: This slice extends an already approved Local CI Gate architecture to a second consumer profile. If implementation changes the toolkit contract, add a fresh cross-family architecture review before merge.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #264 may add an AIS Local CI Gate profile, focused profile tests, runbook documentation, progress/closeout evidence, and no downstream product changes.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Merge this planning/scope branch so the implementation execution scope is trusted from main, then implement on branch feat/issue-264-ai-integration-services-local-ci-profile.
blocked_on: planning PR merged so implementation scope is trusted from main

material_deviation_rules: If implementation requires changes outside the declared implementation scope, update the scope through a planning PR before continuing.
If AIS lacks a required command or dependency state, keep the profile check out or mark it scoped rather than adding downstream scripts from governance.
If live dogfood requires AIS product-repo edits, open a separate AIS issue-backed PR.
If the profile changes the Local CI Gate runner contract, add cross-family review evidence before merge.
Do not alter dirty shared hldpro-governance or AIS checkouts.
approved_by: operator instruction: create GH issue and upload the code / files needed to implement
